behavior_summary: Orchestrates a short audiovisual 'body part' effect: creates a central sprite + SFX, spawns multiple fragment item instances around a source coordinate, animates/throws those fragments, optionally locks player control while the effect runs, waits for an associated NPC to be idle, then repositions that NPC and updates the original item's display frame.
Uses type-specific helper routines to decide how fragments behave (how they are hurled, what frames/timers they run, and whether they spawn further animated pieces).
Employs short-lived spawned timers/processes as delays between visual steps and to poll NPC busy state, ensuring the sequence runs asynchronously but in a controlled order.
notable_control_flow: Guard/early-return: read of an indirect byte at obj_ptr+02; if == 1 the main routine returns immediately (acts as a usage/activation guard).
At start the routine calls setAvatarInStasis(1) to likely lock the player during the sequence.
Busy-wait loop: while Npc::isBusy(obj_ptr) is true the script spawns a short timer (FREE::1C19/FREE::1A96) and suspends; repeats until not busy.

Sequence of steps: read source coords (Item::getX/Y/Z) -> createSprite/playSFX -> repeatedly Item::create + Item::pop at offsets -> process Item::use on each fragment -> Camera::setCenterOn + METHOD::0113 -> Npc::getMap + Npc::teleport -> Item::setFrame on the source.

Helpers are called for type-dependent behavior: U8P_BODYPART::BODYPART::0270 (hurl logic), U8P_BODYPART::BODYPART::0431 (frame/timer/SFX logic), U8P_BODYPART::BODYPART::0577 (create/pop/destroy/fall combo), and U8P_BODYPART::BODYPART::05DC (fragment animation loop).
open_questions: What exact in-game event triggers BODYPART::0080 (item use, NPC limb sever, scripted cutscene)? — inferred but not definitive.
What is the precise semantics and name of the indirect byte at obj_ptr+02 (guard field) — is it a 'used' flag, 'severed' flag, or restart guard?
What are the concrete sprite IDs, SFX IDs, and METHOD::0113 semantics (UI transition/fade/other)?
Is avatar stasis released inside this script or elsewhere (script sets stasis on start but no explicit release observed in the excerpt)?

main_function.name: U8P_BODYPART::0080
main_function.purpose: When activated, run a multi-step effect that spawns and animates body-part fragments around a source object, play associated SFX/visuals, wait for the target NPC to be idle, then teleport/reposition the NPC and update the original item's frame. Intended to represent an item/NPC body-part event such as severing or item activation.
entry_conditions: Called with an object pointer parameter (obj_ptr at BP+06h) which is used as the source for coords and method calls.
Immediate guard: returns if the indirect byte at obj_ptr+02 equals 1 (guard prevents re-entry or redundant activation).
Invoked in contexts where the source object/NPC is present and may need to be animated/teleported — likely an item 'use' or NPC event.

function_map.name: U8P_BODYPART::0080
function_map.role: Coordinator/process that runs the complete body-part spawn/animation/teleport sequence.

function_map.name: U8P_BODYPART::0270
function_map.role: Determine velocities/angles and invoke Item::hurl for fragments based on Item::getType; introduces short timed delays before hurling.

function_map.name: U8P_BODYPART::0431
function_map.role: Handle frame changes, SFX, short delays and may spawn further fragment animation (calls BODYPART::05DC for piece drops for some types).

function_map.name: U8P_BODYPART::0577
function_map.role: Create a new item of a given type, pop it at the source coordinates, destroy the original, and call Item::fall on the new instance (replace-and-fall behavior).

function_map.name: U8P_BODYPART::05DC
function_map.role: Animate a dropped fragment over time: set initial frame and run a timed loop to increment/adjust frames with delays, cycling until the animation completes.

function_map.name: Item::*
function_map.role: Item methods invoked (create, pop, use, hurl, setFrame, getX/getY/getZ/getType/getFrame, destroy, fall) to allocate, position, animate and remove fragment items.

function_map.name: Npc::*
function_map.role: Npc methods (isBusy, getMap, teleport) used to query readiness and reposition the NPC after the effect.

internal_calls.purpose: Delegate per-type hurl/throw behavior for spawned fragments (compute velocities, play short timers then call Item::hurl).
Drive frame/SFX/timer behavior for certain fragment types and possibly spawn BODYPART::05DC to animate drops.
Spawn a timed loop to animate a dropped piece (frame cycling/aging).
Use to replace an item at source coords and trigger falling behavior for the replacement when appropriate.

external_calls.purpose: Place avatar into stasis/locked state at sequence start to restrict player control during effect.
Poll whether the target NPC is busy; used in a loop with spawned timers to wait until the NPC can be teleported/acted upon.
Read source coordinates/elevation from the object that triggered the effect for positioning fragments and final teleport target.
Create a temporary visual sprite/effect centered near the source coordinate as part of the effect.
Play a sound effect associated with the visual effect (attached to the source object).
Spawn short timer/processes used both as small delays between steps and to implement polling/wait behavior; common for asynchronous sequencing.
Allocate fragment item instances, place them at computed offsets and immediately run their 'use' processing to initialize animations/behaviors.
Launch fragment items with computed velocities/angles to produce scattered-throw effects.
Center camera on the relevant target (PID/object) to focus the player view during the effect.
Trigger a small UI/visual transition or callback after centering the camera (exact semantics inferred as a transition/fade).
Ensure correct map context and teleport/reposition the NPC to a spot slightly offset from the source after fragments are spawned.
Update the original item's display frame to reflect the changed/used/severed state when the sequence completes.

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Orchestrates a short audiovisual 'body part' effect: creates a central sprite + SFX, spawns multiple fragment item instances around a source coordinate, animates/throws those fragments, optionally locks player control while the effect runs, waits for an associated NPC to be idle, then repositions that NPC and updates the original item's display frame.
